message:
fix(db): fix cache drain race, coalesce lock holding, schema migration strategy, and query correctness

ReadCache.drain_touches now clears the has_pending flag BEFORE draining shards
instead of after, closing a race window where a concurrent enqueue between the
drain and the flag-clear would be silently lost. The new ordering ensures any
enqueue racing the drain either gets drained or re-sets the flag for the next
pass.

ShardedCoalesceMap now drops the shard mutex guard immediately after cloning
the Arc (for Joiner) or inserting/removing entries (for Leader), rather than
holding the lock across the entire coalesce operation. This reduces contention
under high-concurrency workloads.

The inbox_stats INSERT trigger is no longer stored as a migration row. Instead,
it is applied idempotently at runtime after migrations complete via
ensure_inbox_stats_insert_trigger_compat(). This avoids PRIMARY KEY violations
that some SQLite engines surface when running UPSERT syntax inside trigger
bodies. The v12 migration now only records the DROP, and the compatible
INSERT OR IGNORE + UPDATE form is recreated post-migration for both full and
base (FrankenConnection) migration paths.

Additional fixes:
- "no healthy backup was found" added to corruption error detection patterns
- create_file_reservations uses IMMEDIATE transaction (not CONCURRENT) to
  serialize reservation checks and prevent TOCTOU races
- LIKE fallback search wraps WHERE clause in parentheses to prevent operator
  precedence bugs with complex conditions
- saturating_add used for reservation expiry calculation
- sync.rs: let-chain syntax, .clone() over .to_string(), formatting cleanup,
  "Message"/"Project" entity strings changed from String to &str

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
